news.summary: Global trade tensions have sparked investor interest in alternative assets such as Bitcoin and tokenized real-world assets (RWAs) due to concerns over the long-term stability of the financial system. Despite US President Donald Trump's announcement of a 90-day pause on higher reciprocal tariffs, tariffs on Chinese goods were escalated to 125%. This tariff escalation is viewed as a significant turning point for global markets, revealing deeper fractures in the monetary system. Tokenized gold trading volume surged to a two-year high, surpassing $1 billion, while onchain RWAs reached an all-time high of $20 billion, with tokenized private credit accounting for $12.7 billion. Analysts suggest that RWAs could reach a $50 billion all-time high by 2025. While some believe the tariffs are a US bargaining tool and not a lasting policy shift, others see them as negotiations posturing. The recovery of global risk assets, including the crypto market, may be influenced by the tone of the negotiations. Nansen analysts predict a 70% chance of the crypto market bottoming by June 2025.
news.headline: Trump’s tariff escalation highlights global financial system rifts as trade tensions persist. Tokenized assets see increased interest. Tariffs on Chinese goods rise from 104% to 125%. Top tokenized gold trading volume hits $1B; onchain RWAs reach $20B. Bitcoin's lack of upside may boost RWAs to $50B by 2025. Tariffs viewed as US bargaining tactic, not long-term policy shift. Recovery of global risk assets, including crypto, hinges on tariff negotiations. The potential to bottom by June 2025 seen.

news.summary: Protagonist Therapeutics, Inc. revealed groundbreaking new findings on their pill designed to address moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis in a recent announcement. The Phase 3 trial encompassed both adolescents and adults, marking a significant advancement in the development of the treatment. This milestone emerged as part of the partnership with Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ), under the ICONIC program featuring icotrokinra (JNJ-2113). Data showcased at the 2025 World Congress of Pediatric Dermatology (WCPD) Annual Meeting demonstrated that adolescents treated with daily icotrokinra exhibited notably improved rates of clear or nearly clear skin by Week 16 compared to those on a placebo regimen. Notably, no new safety concerns were reported in the study. For more detailed information, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Protagonist and Johnson & Johnson report positive results for psoriasis oral pill study, achieving higher clear skin rates in adolescents with no safety issues. Study conducted on pill icotrokinra (JNJ-2113) in Phase 3 trial.

news.headline: Picarro's Emissions360™ wins top award for software + cloud product. Key features include real-time analytics, announced on April 10, 2025.

news.summary: Constellation Brands Inc (STZ) reported fourth-quarter earnings with net sales of $2.16 billion, surpassing estimates of $2.13 billion. Beer segment sales remained stable at $1.70 billion while Wine and Spirits sales rose 11% to $459.8 million. Beer shipment volume dropped by 1.8% while Wine and Spirits increased by 3.5%. Gross profit rose by 7% to $1.11 billion but the company reported an operating loss of $(150.3) million, a decline from $629.4 million the previous year. The adjusted EPS of $2.63 exceeded the estimated $2.28. Operating cash flow was $3.2 billion, a 13% Y/Y increase, with a free cash flow of $1.9 billion, a 28% Y/Y growth. The company had $68.1 million in cash by February 28 and 48 million hectoliters of production capacity in Mexico by the end of fiscal 2025. Additionally, Constellation declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.02 per share, payable on May 15.
news.headline: Constellation Brands reports Q4 earnings: net sales $2.16B, Beer sales $1.7B, Wine/Spirits $459.8M. Operating loss $(150.3)M. Adjusted EPS $2.63 beats $2.28 estimate. Cash flow: $3.2B increase YoY, $1.9B free cash flow YoY. 48M hectoliters production capacity in Mexico. Quarterly dividend: $1.02 per share, 1% increase.

news.summary: On April 11th, Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE: WFC) will reveal their Q1 financial results before the market opens. Analysts predict earnings of $1.23 per share, an increase from $1.20 per share in the previous year. The company anticipates quarterly revenue of $20.76 billion, slightly lower than the $20.86 billion reported in the same period last year. Recently, Wells Fargo announced the conclusion of its 2021 consent order with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency regarding loss mitigation practices. Following this news, Wells Fargo's stock rose by 6.9% to close at $66.33 on Wednesday. For more details and analyst ratings, visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.
news.headline: Wells Fargo to report Q1 earnings on April 11; Analysts expect $1.23 EPS, $20.76 billion revenue. Stocks rose 6.9% to $66.33.

news.summary: Innoblative is gearing up to begin a feasibility study to assess the safety and efficacy of its SIRA® RFA Electrosurgical Device, which has been designated as an FDA Breakthrough Device. The study will focus on patients undergoing breast-conserving surgery. Breast cancer holds the top spot as the most frequently diagnosed cancer worldwide, affecting millions of individuals. The study will be conducted in Chicago and aims to gather crucial data on the device's performance in this clinical setting.
news.headline: Innoblative gets U.S. IDE for SIRA® RFA device for breast cancer, starting feasibility study. Breast cancer is top global cancer.

news.summary: A popular pet supplement brand in the UK, YuMOVE, recently transitioned its subscriber experience to Ordergroove's enterprise platform based in New York. news.headline: YuMOVE, UK's top pet supplement brand, shifts to Ordergroove platform to improve experience for hundreds of thousands of subscribers.
